Featured Device

Motu Ultralite-mk3 Hybrid

Hybrid audio interface for professional music production.

Official MOTU Page Back to Library

MOTU UltraLite-mk3

Section-based notes for routing, CueMix, converters, monitoring, and hardware setup.

  • Overview
  • Hybrid FireWire/USB2
  • Mic/Guitar Inputs
  • Comprehensive I/O
  • Mixing and effects
  • CueMix FX software
  • Classic reverb
  • Modeled analog EQ
  • Vintage compression
  • Tuner & analysis tools
  • Clock and sync
  • MIDI
  • Expansion & drivers
  • Feature Summary

MOTU product page

Table of contents

  • Important: Run the UltraLite-mk3 Software Installer First
  • Installing the UltraLite-mk3 Hybrid Hardware (excerpt)

  • Channel Page Parameters (excerpt)
  • Cubase, Nuendo, Live and Other ASIO Software

Features

Click a feature to expand details.

CueMix FX — on‑board DSP mixer & effects

On‑board 16‑bus (8 stereo) CueMix FX with 32‑bit floating point DSP for zero‑latency monitoring, 7‑band parametric EQ, compression (including the Leveler™) and Classic Reverb.

See the CueMix FX section for full details.

Hybrid Connectivity (USB2 & FireWire)

Supports both hi‑speed USB2 and FireWire A connections. FireWire supports bus‑powered operation; USB requires the included DC adapter when not bus‑powered.

High‑quality converters & sample rates

24‑bit audio path with converters supporting professional sample rates (up to 192 kHz on select I/O). Use the sample rate setting in MOTU Audio Console.

Multiple analog I/O & S/PDIF

Provides 6 analog inputs (including 2 front-panel combo inputs with preamps) and 10 balanced quarter‑inch analog outputs, plus RCA S/PDIF in/out for digital I/O.

Low‑latency monitoring & Samples Per Buffer

Direct monitoring via CueMix FX provides near‑zero latency. Adjust Samples Per Buffer in the MOTU driver or host for lowest monitoring latency in Ableton Live.

MOTU Audio Console

Cross‑platform control application to enable/disable I/O, set clock source, sample rate, routing, and access CueMix FX parameters.

Open via the Hardware Setup / MOTU Audio Console entry when using Ableton Live.

Important: Run the UltraLite-mk3 Software Installer First

INSTALL THE ULTRALITE-MK3 SOFTWARE FIRST!

If Windows asks you to locate the drivers and you’ve already connected the UltraLite-mk3 to your computer and switched it on, Windows may prompt you to locate the drivers on disk. If this happens:

  1. Cancel the driver search.
  2. Switch off the UltraLite-mk3.
  3. Run the UltraLite-mk3 Software Installer.

Installing the UltraLite-mk3 software

To install the UltraLite-mk3 software, insert the MOTU Universal Audio Installer CD and follow the on-screen directions. The UltraLite-mk3 ships with the following software and drivers:

  • MOTU Audio Console — access to hardware settings.
  • CueMix FX — no-latency monitoring, mixing and processing.
  • MOTU ASIO Driver — multi-channel I/O for ASIO hosts.
  • MOTU WDM Driver — multichannel I/O for WDM hosts.
  • MOTU MIDI Driver — MIDI I/O support.

Installing the UltraLite-mk3 Hybrid Hardware (excerpt)

Connect the UltraLite-mk3 interface

The UltraLite-mk3 Hybrid provides both a FireWire A and a hi-speed USB2 connector. Use either port to connect to your computer.

Which should I use: FireWire or USB2?

If your computer lacks FireWire, use USB2. Note: bus-powered operation requires FireWire — USB2 requires the included DC power supply.

Connect audio inputs and outputs

The interface exposes:

  • 10 balanced quarter-inch analog outputs
  • 6 balanced quarter-inch analog inputs
  • 2 XLR/quarter-inch “combo” analog inputs with preamps
  • 1 pair of RCA S/PDIF in and out
Mic / instrument inputs

Connect mics, guitars or instruments to the XLR/TRS combo using appropriate cables. Use the front-panel TRIM encoders to set levels.

Phantom power

Push and hold the corresponding front-panel TRIM encoder to toggle phantom power for condenser microphones.

Trim and 20 dB pad

Front-panel trims: XLR mic input (+60 dB) and TRS instrument input (+32 dB). The XLR mic input has a 20 dB pad toggled by pressing the TRIM encoder.

CueMix FX (verbatim)

CUEMIX FX 32-BIT FLOATING POINT MIXING AND EFFECTS

All UltraLite-mk3 inputs and outputs can be routed to the on-board CueMix FX 16-bus (8 stereo) digital mixer driven by hardware-based DSP with 32-bit floating point precision. The mixer allows you to apply no-latency effects processing to inputs, outputs or busses directly in the UltraLite-mk3 hardware, independent of the computer. Effects can even be applied when the UltraLite-mk3 is operating stand-alone (without a computer) as a complete portable mixer. Input signals to the computer can be recorded wet, dry, or dry with a wet monitor mix (for musicians during recording, for example).

Effects include reverb, parametric EQ and compression/limiting. The UltraLite-mk3’s Classic Reverb™ provides five different room types, three frequency bands with adjustable crossover points, shelf filtering and reverb lengths up to 60‑seconds. Two forms of compression are supplied: a standard compressor with conventional threshold/ratio/attack/release/gain controls and the Leveler™, an accurate model of the legendary LA‑2A optical compressor, which provides vintage, musical automatic gain control.

CueMix FX also provides 7‑band parametric EQ modeled after British analog console EQs, featuring 4 filter styles (gain/Q profiles). Low‑pass and high‑pass filters with slopes from 6 to 36 dB are included. The EQ employs high‑precision 64‑bit floating point processing.

The effects architecture lets you apply EQ and compression on every input and output (24 channels total), with DSP resources for at least one band of parametric EQ and compression per channel at 48 kHz. DSP resources are allocated dynamically and a DSP meter in the CueMix FX software helps monitor usage. Each input, output and mix bus provides a send to the Classic Reverb processor, which then feeds reverb returns to mix busses and outputs, with a selectable split point to prevent send/return feedback loops.

Front-panel Operation — LCD modes & menus (verbatim)

The multi-purpose backlit LCD provides metering for all inputs and outputs and access to the built-in mixer and settings. There are eight independent stereo mix busses. The four knobs to the right of the LCD correspond to four labeled sections. Push the PARAMETER knob to cycle the three LCD modes: METER, CUEMIX, and SETUP.

METER mode: level meters for each input/output. CUEMIX mode: program the on‑board CueMix FX mixer — use the CHANNEL knob to select inputs/outputs/busses and PAGE, PARAM, VALUE knobs to edit settings. SETUP mode: general settings via the PARAMETER and VALUE knobs.

OUTPUTS MENU MIXES MENU REVERB MENU

Analog 3-4, etc.

SLOPE
FREQ
LF (Low w/shelf)
ENABLE
TYPE
FREQ
GAIN
WIDTH
... (full parameter list preserved)
SPLIT

Ableton Live & ASIO

OVERVIEW — The UltraLite-mk3 includes an ASIO driver that provides multi-channel I/O and sample-accurate synchronization for ASIO-compatible hosts.

What is ASIO? ASIO stands for Audio Streaming Input and Output.

In Ableton Live, open Preferences → Audio. Choose ASIO as the Driver Type and select MOTU Audio ASIO as the Audio Device. Use the Input Config and Output Config buttons to enable channels, and use the Hardware Setup button to open MOTU Audio Console.

Before running Ableton Live, launch MOTU Audio Console to enable desired hardware inputs/outputs — only enabled I/O will be available to the host.

Reducing monitoring latency

Samples Per Buffer — The Samples Per Buffer setting can be used to reduce the delay — or monitoring latency — that you hear when live audio is patched through your UltraLite-mk3 hardware and Ableton Live. For example, live instruments patched through the UltraLite-mk3 and monitored in Live may require lower buffer sizes. See chapter 9, “Reducing Monitoring Latency” for complete details.

Direct monitoring

The Direct Monitoring option allows you to monitor inputs directly in the UltraLite-mk3 hardware with near zero latency and no drain on the computer. When enabled, Ableton Live will use the UltraLite-mk3’s CueMix FX monitoring features whenever you use Live’s monitoring features.

Built for fast lookups, cleaner routing decisions, and practical studio recall.
